questions.question: Почему Flink CEP использует NFA, а не DFA?

options.text: DFA не существует для regex-like patterns

NFA имеет polynomial size в number of states; DFA через subset construction exponentially blows up. Plus oneOrMore с unbounded length не закрывается finite-state DFA. IterativeCondition требует non-determinism с stack-like state. AFTER MATCH SKIP variants natural как multiple active computation states
Это historical choice без особой причины

questions.explanation: DFA конвертация regex → exponential blow-up из subset construction. NFA polynomial, но с multiple active states одновременно. Для CEP: (1) oneOrMore допускает unbounded sequences — не finite-state; (2) IterativeCondition с running aggregations требует access к matched events — это не regular language; (3) AFTER MATCH SKIP variants естественно expressed через non-determinism. Trade-off: NFA slower per event (check multiple active states), но manageable state size. DFA was non-starter для CEP в general.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — секция NFA vs DFA

questions.question: Что такое Shared Buffer и DeweyVersion в CEP engine?

options.text: Shared Buffer — DAG-style storage matched events с versioning через DeweyVersion (hierarchical dot-separated paths). Это позволяет multiple ComputationStates шарить events efficiently — O(N) вместо O(N×M) naive duplication
Это in-memory cache Flink job graph
Buffer для network shuffles между TaskManager-ами

questions.explanation: Shared Buffer — ключевая optimization CEP engine. Naive: каждая ComputationState хранит свою copy событий — O(N×M) для M parallel matches, N events each. С Shared Buffer + DeweyVersion (например 1.2.1 ⊂ 1.2 ⊂ 1) — events shared via hierarchical versioning. Когда ComputationState expires (timeout, terminal), Flink prunes events не reachable from active versions. Reduces storage от O(N×M) к O(distinct_event_path_lengths) — обычно O(N).
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — секция ComputationState и Shared Buffer

questions.question: Pattern с followedByAny + oneOrMore + allowCombinations имеет какую state complexity?

options.text: O(2^N) exponential — allowCombinations enumerates все subsets matching events. 2^20 = 1M parallel partial matches для всего 20 events. Combined с followedByAny это NEVER на production без tight bounds

questions.explanation: Triple-combo combinatorial explosion: followedByAny — non-det relaxed, каждый matching A создаёт state с каждым subsequent B → cartesian; oneOrMore — variable-length, growing per event; allowCombinations — все 2^N subsets matching events. Combined: для N=20 = 1M ComputationStates, каждая с shared events pointers. State blow-up, checkpoint stall, JVM OOM. Production safe alternative: followedBy (single match per A), bound через times(min, max), MATCH_RECOGNIZE с tight WITHIN, или custom state machine.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — секция Why complex patterns expensive

questions.question: Изменение pattern (добавление state, изменение predicate) preserves savepoint compatibility — можно restart с new pattern на old savepoint.
questions.explanation: False. Pattern change → NFA structure changes → savepoint state structure не matches new NFA → deserialization error на restart. Это hard constraint NFA serialization. Production practice: pattern change = breaking deployment. Drain existing patterns (stop without savepoint, или explicit timeout window для full processing), deploy с new code и clean state. Альтернативы: dual-write (старая + новая jobs во время migration), explicit state migration code. Это major operational consideration — pattern changes treat as schema breaking change.
